rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY UNOBSKEY VS. SIMON MICHAEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 18, 2021, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT GARY MILLAR, GAIL SABIN's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Continued to September 20, 2021 per stipulation and order. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: YING WANG VS. ANTHONY GO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 18, 2021, line 2. DEFENDANT ANTHONY GO, RAYMOND GO, QUAN YUNG GO REVOKABLE TRUST's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. OVERRULED and DENIED. Demurrer is overruled. It is based on an argument that does not appear on the face of the complaint of judicially noticeable documents. (The Court notes that Defendant did not ask for judicial notice of the document attempted to introduce it via counsel declaration). Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is DENIED. No authority is cited for the proposition that such request is procedurally proper within this action. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES VS. STEVEN CONLEY, SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Northridge Cooperative Homes, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Order Granting Defendant Steven Conley, Sr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 18, 2021, line 3. PLAINTIFF NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES' Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Order Granting Defendant Steven Conley, Sr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment. DENIED. The motion is based on purported mistake or excusable neglect regarding failure to file a Change of Address. Proof of Service indicates that the Motion for Summary Judgment was served at the new address; therefore, failure to file a Change of Address with the Court did not cause entry of MSJ order and judgment thereon. Furthermore, no authority is cited for the proposition that relief under CCP 1008 is available after entry of judgment. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
